message:
feat(robot): add batch pane output retrieval via --panes, --all, and state --include-text

Extends the Robot Mode API to support batch pane output retrieval, reducing
the number of round-trips needed for swarm monitoring from O(n) to O(1):

crates/frankenterm/src/main.rs:
- robot state: add --include-text flag that inlines per-pane tail output into
  the state response, with --tail and --escapes parameters. When set, each pane
  object in the response includes a "text" field with the most recent N lines.
- robot get-text: add --panes <id,id,...> for explicit pane list and --all for
  all active panes. These are mutually exclusive with the positional pane_id.
  Selector validation ensures exactly one of pane_id, --panes, or --all is used.
- Switch from default_wezterm_handle() to wezterm_handle_from_config(&config) in
  both state and get-text code paths, ensuring socket sharding and custom socket
  paths from config are respected.
- Add 3 CLI parse tests: robot state --include-text flags, robot get-text
  --panes selector, robot get-text --all selector

README.md:
- Document new batch operations in the Robot Mode quick reference section:
  state --include-text --tail, get-text --panes, get-text --all

docs/cli-reference.md:
- Update robot state and get-text signatures to reflect new flag syntax

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -345,7 +345,10 @@ Use `--format toon` for token-efficient output and `ft robot help` for the full
 
 ```bash
 ft robot state               # All panes as JSON
-ft robot get-text <id> --tail 50      # Pane output as JSON
+ft robot state --include-text --tail 20  # Pane metadata + per-pane tail output
+ft robot get-text <id> --tail 50      # Single pane output as JSON
+ft robot get-text --panes 0,1,2 --tail 20  # Batch pane output in one call
+ft robot get-text --all --tail 10     # Batch all active panes in one call
 ft robot send <id> "<text>" # Send input (with policy)
 ft robot send <id> "<text>" --dry-run  # Preview without executing
 ft robot wait-for <id> <rule_id>       # Wait for pattern

@@ -150,8 +150,8 @@ ft undo
 Robot mode uses a stable envelope and mirrors MCP schemas.
 
 ```bash
-ft robot state [--domain <name>] [--agent <type>]
-ft robot get-text <pane_id> [--tail <n>] [--escapes]
+ft robot state [--include-text] [--tail <n>] [--escapes]
+ft robot get-text <pane_id>|--panes <id,id,...>|--all [--tail <n>] [--escapes]
 ft robot send <pane_id> "<text>" [--dry-run] [--wait-for "<pat>"] [--timeout-secs <n>]
 ft robot wait-for <pane_id> "<pat>" [--timeout-secs <n>] [--regex]
 ft robot search "<fts query>" [--pane <id>] [--since <epoch_ms>] [--until <epoch_ms>] [--limit <n>] [--snippets[=<true|false>]] [--mode <lexical|semantic|hybrid>]
